topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed with an almost aggressive intensity, reflecting off the polished floor tiles. Liam smoothed down his already impeccable tie for the tenth time, his palms slick against the crisp fabric. He stared at the application form, the lines of his handwriting feeling suddenly alien and inadequate. Across the small table, Maya was casually flipping through her own resume, a faint, almost imperceptible smile playing on her lips. Every time she shifted, the movement seemed to draw the attention of the entire room, and Liam felt a cold knot tighten in his stomach. He reread the job description—the exact same requirements, the identical set of responsibilities. A sudden, sharp intake of breath made him acutely aware of the silence around him. He wanted to bolt, to disappear into the beige cubicle walls, but his feet remained rooted.

[story 2]

"Are you sure about this, Alex?" Sarah whispered, her voice thin as tracing paper. She leaned in conspiratorially, her eyes wide and fixed on the shared document spread between them. Alex’s hands were shaking so badly he had to grip the edge of the desk until his knuckles were white. The air in the shared workspace felt thick, suffocating. He glanced at Maya, who was calmly reviewing her portfolio, her posture radiating an unnerving composure. Every minute detail of the application felt magnified under the scrutiny of their mutual competition. A strange pressure built behind Alex’s eyes, a dizzying sensation that made the edges of the room swim. He swallowed hard, the dryness in his throat making the action painful.

[story 3]

I kept glancing over at Ben. He was sitting there, utterly relaxed, sipping lukewarm coffee as if he were waiting for a bus, not facing down this precipice. My heart was hammering against my ribs, a frantic, uneven rhythm that echoed loudly in my ears. The ink on the application felt like molten lead beneath my fingertips. I tried to focus on the required qualifications, but my vision kept blurring at the edges. Every casual glance Ben offered my way felt like a spotlight, illuminating my foolishness. I could feel a distinct clamminess spreading across my neck, and I desperately wanted to look away, to somehow dissolve into the background noise of the bustling office, but I couldn't break my gaze from the shared document.
